channel.title: AI Agents on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in AI Agents on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: security: Defense in depth for autonomous AI agents
item.description:
Microsoft says autonomous AI agents require updated defense-in-depth controls centered on application-layer design, identity, and human oversight. The post frames agent security as an evolution of existing defense-in-depth practices rather than a new model.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: When prompts become shells: RCE vulnerabilities in AI agent frameworks
item.description:
Microsoft says new research found prompt injection issues in AI agent frameworks can lead to remote code execution. The post outlines affected areas and recommends securing agents against these flaws.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: dotnet: Durable Workflows in the Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now supports durable AI agent workflows using the Durable Task runtime. The post shows a progression from in-process console apps to parallel agent execution and Azure Functions hosting for serverless runs.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Microsoft Agent Framework – Building Blocks for AI Part 3
item.description:
Part 3 of the Microsoft Agent Framework series shows how to build .NET AI agents using tools, multi-turn conversations, memory, and graph-based workflows. It ties together the earlier parts into the framework’s main building blocks for agent development.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: Microsoft Agent 365, now generally available, expands capabilities and integrations
item.description:
Microsoft announced general availability of Agent 365 and previewed new features for discovering and managing shadow AI agents, including local agents such as OpenClaw and Claude Code.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: ai: Introducing Toolboxes in Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry introduces Toolboxes in public preview as a way to curate, configure, and reuse tools across AI agents. It is intended to reduce duplicated tool wiring, authentication, credentials, and integration code across frameworks and runtimes.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: ai: From Local to Production: The Complete Developer Journey for Building, Composing, and Deploying AI Agents
item.description:
Microsoft announces v1.0 of Microsoft Agent Framework and general availability of the Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code. The post frames these releases as part of a workflow for building, composing, and deploying AI agents from local development to production.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: security: The agentic SOC—Rethinking SecOps for the next decade
item.description:
Microsoft describes a future SOC model where autonomous defense handles machine-speed response, agents provide context and coordination, and human analysts focus on judgment, risk, and outcomes.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Dependabot alerts are now assignable to AI agents for remediation
item.description:
Dependabot alerts can now be assigned to AI coding agents for remediation, including Copilot, Claude, and Codex. This is intended for vulnerabilities that require code changes beyond a simple dependency version update.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Governing AI agent behavior: Aligning user, developer, role, and organizational intent
item.description:
The report discusses how to govern AI agent behavior by aligning user, developer, role, and organizational intent for secure enterprise AI adoption.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: CTI-REALM: A new benchmark for end-to-end detection rule generation with AI agents
item.description:
Microsoft introduced CTI-REALM, an open-source benchmark for evaluating AI agents on end-to-end detection engineering. It measures whether agents can turn cyber threat intelligence into validated detection rules.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: dotnet: Accelerating .NET MAUI Development with AI Agents
item.description:
Microsoft says custom-built AI agents are being used to improve the .NET MAUI contribution workflow, with reported 50–70% reductions in issue resolution time. The post also claims increased test coverage and code quality.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: SQL MCP Server
item.description:
Azure announced a public preview of SQL MCP Server, an MCP connector for production databases. It is part of Data API Builder and is intended to provide a simple, predictable, and secure way to connect AI agents to data workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates
